Projects.Title: example-project
Projects.Current Focus: Bugfix discipline, release notes, task clarity, reusable debugging playbooks
Projects.AI Summary: Example project record for Feishu Dev Hub. Replace this with the real project summary after provisioning.
Projects.Search Keywords: example-project bugfix release tasks pitfalls playbooks

Areas.Title: Core Application
Areas.AI Summary: Primary product behavior and user-facing workflows.

Areas.Title: Release / Main Push
Areas.AI Summary: Release evidence, protected branch workflow, and knowledge writeback gate.

Pitfalls.Title: Knowledge base should not store secrets
Pitfalls.Trigger Condition: AI writes project context, bug evidence, logs, or configuration into Feishu.
Pitfalls.Wrong Approach: Paste access tokens, app secrets, private keys, credentials, or raw environment files into Base or Docs.
Pitfalls.Correct Approach: Store only facts, evidence summaries, safe command names, file paths, and links to internal artifacts.
Pitfalls.Search Keywords: secrets credentials Feishu Dev Hub knowledge writeback

Pitfalls.Title: Main push needs release evidence
Pitfalls.Trigger Condition: A branch is pushed to main or master.
Pitfalls.Wrong Approach: Push first and reconstruct verification evidence later.
Pitfalls.Correct Approach: Write a Release record before the push or leave an outbox item with verification commands and result.
Pitfalls.Search Keywords: main push release evidence kb-updated outbox receipt

Playbooks.Title: Bugfix writeback checklist
Playbooks.Scenario: A bug was investigated and changed code or configuration.
Playbooks.Diagnosis Order: Search old Pitfalls/Bugfixes -> collect symptom evidence -> identify root cause -> fix -> run verification -> write Bugfix and AI Run.
Playbooks.Must Check Evidence: Relevant old records, changed files, verification command output, regression risk.
Playbooks.Forbidden Actions: Do not mark a bug fixed without recording what proved the fix.

Decisions.Title: Base is the structured source of truth
Decisions.Decision: Feishu Base stores structured records. Docs store long-form context. Whiteboard stores visual maps with text summaries linked back to Base.
Decisions.Context: Humans need a browsable workspace; AI agents need searchable records and stable field names.
Decisions.Consequences: Agents search Base first, then open linked Docs or Whiteboards only when needed.
Decisions.Search Keywords: Feishu Base source of truth Docs Whiteboard AI searchable

Project Facts.Title: Project Facts store current implementation truth
Project Facts.Fact: Project-specific current truths and retired paths live in Project Facts, not in public skills.
Project Facts.Current Truth: Agents should search Project Facts before relying on old code paths or stale architecture notes.
Project Facts.Retired Paths: Project-specific retired endpoints, modules, or workflows should be recorded here.
Project Facts.Review Trigger: Review after architecture changes, PR review feedback, or repeated stale-agent behavior.
Project Facts.AI Summary: Project Facts prevent stale AI behavior by storing current implementation truth and retired paths.
Project Facts.Search Keywords: project facts current truth retired paths stale AI
